content_en:
"An authentic hadith recorded by Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi and others JZ

much greater purpose: the worship of Allah and His pleasure in the Hereafter. ]

The Ruling Concerning the Prayer and The Number of Prayers

The obligatory prayers are also divided into two categories: prayers obligatory upon each and every individual and prayers only obligatory on the community as a whole.

The prayers that are obligatory upon each individual are those obligatory upon every legally capable man or woman; these are the five daily prayers. Allah says,

“Verily, the prayers are enjoined on the believers at stated times” (al-Nisaa 103). Allah also says,

| gids iy AYics pall A Ad Cpyaloee AU I gcd YY byl vy KB yd EMSs BSN 1 Sly BOCA

“And they have been commanded no more than this: to

worship Allah, offering Him sincere devotion, being true (in

faith), and to establish regular prayer, and to pay the zakaat,

and that is the religion right and straight” (al-Bayyinah 5). The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said,

a off Wy dy of ays Ls le byl - peg abl Bealls BIS ohSfy BDU olay al J Olas)

“Islam is built upon five [pillars]: testifying that there is none worthy of worship except Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, establishing the prayers, giving the zakaat, making the pilgrimage to the House and fasting the month of Ramadhaan.” (Recorded by al-Bukhari.) Naafi ibn al-Azraq said to ibn Abbas [the Companion of the Prophet (peace be upon him)], “Do you find the five [daily] prayers in the Quran. ” He responded, "Yes," and then recited the verse, 6

“So (give) glory to Allah, when you reach the evening [for the sunset and the night prayers] and when you rise in the moming; to Him be praise, in the heavens and on earth; and in the late afternoon and when the day begins to decline [for the noon prayer]” (al-Room 17-18).

In the hadith of the bedouin, he came to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) and said, “Concerning the prayers, what has Allah made obligatory upon us?” The Messenger of Allah answered, “Five prayers.” The bedouin said, “Are there any other prayers obligatory on us?” He replied, “No, unless you do so voluntarily.” (Recorded by alBukhari and Muslim.)

The prayers that are obligatory upon the community as a whole include the other [obligatory] prayers and aspects {related to the prayers], such as the call to prayer, the eclipse prayer and funeral prayer.

When the youth reach the age of seven, they should be ordered to pray. When they are ten, they should be disciplined by a mild beating if they do not pray. This is based on the hadith,

o rove os S07 F or we @ oh 3S “78 2 0 Sor fyee a, “Order your children to pray at the age of seven and beat them

to do so at the age of ten and separate them in their bedding.” (Recorded by Ahmad and Abu Dawood.!)

The Ruling Concerning One Who Denies the Obligation of Prayer

Whoever denies the obligatory nature of prayers has committed an act of unbelief— if he was a person who was not ignorant— even if he performs the prayer. This is because he is belying Allah, His Messenger (peace be upon him) and the consensus of the Muslim Nation. The same ruling applies for the one who abandons the prayer due to being lackadaisical or lazy, even if he believes in its obligatory nature. Allah has said,

* According to al-Albaani, this hadith is basan. See al-Albaani, Sabeeb al-Jaami, vol. 2, pp. 1021-1022—JZ

why arly wh yi oh sally Ce GS pol eye Tg Bad | gall, Ig OY ein JS oil Lyi

“Then fight and slay the pagans wherever you find them, and seize them, beleaguer them, and lie in wait for them in every stratagem (of war); but if they repent, and establish regular prayers and pay the zakaat, then open the way for them: for Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful” (al-Taubah 5). Jaabir (may Allah be pleased with him) narrated that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said,

“Between a man and polytheism (al-shirk) and disbelief (alkufr) is the abandoning of the prayer.” (Recorded by Muslim.)

There are fourteen essential components or arkaan (ols m)) of the prayer which may not be left, not intentionally, forgetfully or ignorantly.!

(1) Standing straight in the obligatory prayer for the one who has the ability to do so.

(2) The opening takbeer which is the statement, “Allahu akbar,” and nothing else suffices for the beginning of the prayer.

' Essential components (arkaan) differ from obligatory acts (waayibaal). If a person fails to perform the essential components, under most circumstances it will be as if he did not perform the act at all. If a person fails to perform the obligatory acts, under some circumstances he may simply have to make up for those acts in another way—JZ

(5) Rising from the bow and standing up straight.

(9) Having calmness and stillness [in each of the acts of the prayer].

(12) Saying the prayers for the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him).

(13) Saying the salutation. This is to say twice, “Assalaam alaikum wa rahmatullaahi (peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah).” It is best not to add, “wa baarakatuhu (and his blessings)” for in the hadith of ibn Masood, he narrated that the Prophet (peace be upon him) would greet on his right, “Assalaam alaikum wa rahmatullaahi (peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah)” and on his left, “Assalaam alaikum wa rahmatullaahi (peace be upon you and the mercy of Allah).” (Recorded by Muslim.)

There are eight obligatory or waajibaat (>>I 5) acts of the prayer:

(2) Saying, “Sami-Allaahu liman hamidah (Allah has heard him who praises Him),” for both the Imam and the one who prays alone.

(3) Saying, “Rabbanaa wa lakal-hamd (Our Lord and to you is the praise).”

' These are the first set of words recited while sitting, beginning with the words altahiyaatu-lilaabi...

(4) Saying, “Subhanna Rabbiyal-Adheem (Exalted and perfect is my Lord, the Great),” at least once while bowing.

(5) Saying, “Subhanna Rabbiyal-Ala (Exalted and perfect is my Lord, the Most High),” at least once while prostrating.

The prayer is nullified if any of these are left intentionally. The prayer is not nullified if they are left forgetfully or ignorantly.

Shuroot (prerequisites, conditions) lexically means a sign. Technically, it refers to something, say X, that Y cannot exist without; however, the mere existence of X does not necessarily mean that Y will occur.

(4) Being at least of the age of discernment.

(9) Being free of any physical impurities [on one’s clothing or place where one is praying].

The timings show the limits for the prayer. The time is a cause mandating the obligation of the prayer and it is one of the prerequisites for the prayer.

The Prophet (peace be upon him) delineated the timings of the five daily prayers in more than one hadith. Ibn Abbaas narrated that the Prophet (peace be upon him) said, “(The angel] Gabriel led me in the prayers at the House [of Allah] on two [days].” Then he mentioned the timings of the prayers and he then said, “Then Gabriel turned to me and said, ‘O Muhammad, this is the timing of the prophets before you and your timings are between these two [extremes that he had shown the Prophet (peace be upon him)].’” Recorded by Abu Dawood.!

The five daily prayers are divided between the day and night. If a person gets his portion of sleep such that he is rested and the moming time is approaching, the time for work and seriousness, the time of the Fajr prayer also comes. This is so that humans consciously realize that they are different from the remainder of the creation. He faces his day and his faith has been increased.

When midday comes, he stops again to ponder over his day with his Lord in the Dhuhr Prayer and in order to correct his deeds of the beginning of the day. As mid-afternoon comes, he prays his prayer, facing with it the rest of his day. Then the Maghrib (Sunset) comes. In front of him is the night and the [Sunset and] Isha Prayers during it will carry him through the night, although it is the time of hidden acts, with light and guidance to the sound path. Furthermore, the prayer, in its varied times, is an opportunity to ponder over Allah’s dominion and His perfection concerning everything that encompasses man in his night and day.

The Time of the Dhubr Prayer. The time of the Dhuhr Prayer begins when the sun passes the meridian or its zenith. The ending of the time for Dhuhr is when the shadow of something is equal to the thing’s length in the afternoon.

' According to al-Albaani, this hadith is basan sabib. See Muhammad Naasir al-Deen alAlbaani, Sabih Sunan Abi Dawood (Riyadh: Maktab al-Tarbiyyah al-Arabi li-Duwal alKhaleej, 1989), vol. 1, p. 79.—JZ

The Time of the Asr Prayer. The beginning of the time for the Asr Prayer is when the shadow of something is equal to its length in the afternoon, as such is when the time for the Dhuhr Prayer comes to an end. As for the ending of the time of the Asr Prayer, the choice time by which to pray it is when the shadow of something is twice its own length. However, if necessary, it may be prayed all the way up until [just before] the sun sets.

The Time ofthe Maghrib Prayer. The beginning of the time for the Maghrib Prayer is when the sun sets. Its final time is when the stars become numerous and form clusters with one another. However, its actual final time, although it is disliked to delay it until this time, is when the twilight is finished.

The Time of the Isha Prayer. Its earliest time is when the twilight has disappeared. Its ending time is when half of the night has passed.

The Time of the Fajr Prayer. Its earliest time is at the second [or true] dawn and its ending time is sunrise.

The Timings for the Prayers in Very Northern or Southern Lands

The lands of extreme latitudes are divided into three categories:

(1) Those lands that are between 45° and 48° north or south of the equator; in these lands, the signs of the day and night are very apparent, of long or short lengths.

(2) Those lands that are between 48° and 66° north or south of the equator; in these lands, some of the signs of the day and night are not apparent during portions of the year. For example, the twilight may continue to exist until nearly the time of Fajr.

(3) Those lands that are from beyond 66° north or south of the equator until the respective poles; in these lands,

the daily signs of night and day are not visible for long periods of the year.

The Rulings Concerning Each of These Lands. As for the lands in category (1), it is obligatory upon their inhabitants to perform the prayers in their specified times, as were described above. As for those lands in category (3), there is no difference of opinion that their inhabitants must estimate or approximate the timings of the prayers. This is analogous to the case of estimating the times of the prayers during the time of the Anti-Messiah (Dajjaal). In the hadith, the Prophet (peace be upon him) was asked how long the anti-Messiah would be on the earth. In the response, he said, “A day like a year.” The Companions asked, “That day which is like a year, is it sufficient for us to pray [the prayer of] a day and night?” He answered, “No, you must estimate [the times for the prayers].” (Recorded by Muslim.) There is a difference of opinion as to how they should approximate the timings. Some say that they should follow the timings of the closest land that has the regular signs of day and night and follow those proper signs for the timings of the prayers. Perhaps that is the strongest opinion. Some say that they should approximate them with the timing of a temperate zone, where the night is treated as twelve hours as is the daytime. Some say that they should estimate the prayers according to the timing of Makkah or Madinah.

As for the lands in the second category, their timings for the prayers other than the Isha and Fajr Prayers will be the same as those in the first category [that is, they pray according to what they see]. As for the Jsha and Fajr Prayers, their ruling is the same as for those in the third category [that is, they must estimate the times for those prayers].

The congregational prayer is from the most important acts of obedience to Allah and one of the greatest acts of worship. Furthermore, it is a great show of togetherness, mercy and equality between the Muslims, as they gather together in a small convention five times in a day and night, ina noble fashion, under one person’s leadership and all facing one direction. The hearts come together and are purified; mercy and relationships are strengthened; and the differences flow away.
content_id:
Hukum Shalat dan Jumlah Shalat: Shalat-shalat wajib juga dibagi menjadi dua kategori: shalat yang wajib bagi setiap individu dan shalat yang hanya wajib bagi komunitas secara keseluruhan.

Shalat yang wajib bagi setiap individu adalah shalat yang wajib bagi setiap laki-laki dan perempuan yang sudah mukallaf; yaitu lima shalat sehari. Allah berfirman: "Sesungguhnya shalat itu adalah kewajiban yang ditentukan waktunya atas orang-orang yang beriman" (an-Nisa 103). Allah juga berfirman: "Dan mereka tidak diperintahkan kecuali agar menyembah Allah dengan memurnikan ketaatan kepada-Nya dalam (menjalankan) agama dengan lurus, dan agar mereka mendirikan shalat dan menunaikan zakat; dan yang demikian itulah agama yang lurus" (al-Bayyinah 5).

Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Islam dibangun di atas lima [rukun]: bersaksi bahwa tidak ada yang berhak disembah kecuali Allah dan bahwa Muhammad adalah Rasulullah, mendirikan shalat, menunaikan zakat, mengerjakan haji ke Baitullah, dan berpuasa di bulan Ramadan." (Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari.)

Dalam hadis orang Badui, ia datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan berkata: "Tentang shalat, apa yang telah Allah wajibkan kepada kami?" Rasulullah menjawab: "Lima shalat." Orang Badui itu berkata: "Apakah ada shalat lain yang wajib atas kami?" Beliau menjawab: "Tidak, kecuali jika kamu melakukannya secara sukarela." (Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Shalat yang wajib bagi komunitas secara keseluruhan mencakup shalat-shalat [wajib] lainnya dan aspek-aspek [yang berkaitan dengan shalat], seperti adzan, shalat gerhana, dan shalat jenazah.

Ketika anak-anak mencapai usia tujuh tahun, mereka harus diperintahkan untuk shalat. Ketika mereka berumur sepuluh tahun, mereka harus dihukum dengan pukulan ringan jika mereka tidak shalat. Ini berdasarkan hadis: "Perintahkan anak-anakmu untuk shalat pada usia tujuh tahun dan pukullah mereka untuk melakukannya pada usia sepuluh tahun, dan pisahkan tempat tidur mereka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad dan Abu Dawud.)

Hukum Orang yang Mengingkari Kewajiban Shalat: Barangsiapa mengingkari kewajiban shalat telah melakukan perbuatan kufur—jika ia bukan orang yang tidak tahu—meskipun ia melaksanakan shalat. Hal ini karena ia mendustakan Allah, Rasul-Nya ﷺ, dan ijmak umat Islam. Hukum yang sama berlaku bagi orang yang meninggalkan shalat karena malas atau lalai, meskipun ia meyakini kewajibannya.

Rukun-rukun shalat ada empat belas yang tidak boleh ditinggalkan, baik dengan sengaja, lupa, maupun karena tidak tahu: (1) Berdiri tegak dalam shalat wajib bagi yang mampu. (2) Takbiratul ihram yaitu ucapan "Allahu Akbar" dan tidak ada yang lain yang mencukupi untuk memulai shalat. (5) Bangun dari ruku dan berdiri tegak. (9) Tuma'ninah [ketenangan dan keheningan] dalam setiap perbuatan shalat. (12) Membaca shalawat atas Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. (13) Mengucapkan salam. Ini adalah mengucapkan dua kali "Assalamu alaikum wa rahmatullah (Semoga keselamatan dan rahmat Allah tercurah atasmu)."

Kewajiban-kewajiban shalat ada delapan: (2) Mengucapkan "Sami'allahu liman hamidah (Allah telah mendengar orang yang memuji-Nya)," bagi imam dan orang yang shalat sendiri. (3) Mengucapkan "Rabbana wa lakal hamd (Wahai Rabb kami, segala puji bagi-Mu)." (4) Mengucapkan "Subhana Rabbiyal Adzim (Maha Suci Rabbku, Yang Maha Agung)," setidaknya sekali saat ruku. (5) Mengucapkan "Subhana Rabbiyal A'la (Maha Suci Rabbku, Yang Maha Tinggi)," setidaknya sekali saat sujud. Shalat batal jika salah satu dari kewajiban ini ditinggalkan dengan sengaja. Shalat tidak batal jika ditinggalkan karena lupa atau tidak tahu.

Syarat-syarat shalat: (4) Minimal telah mencapai usia tamyiz (dapat membedakan). (9) Bebas dari najis fisik [pada pakaian atau tempat seseorang shalat].

Waktu-waktu shalat menunjukkan batas-batas waktu shalat. Waktu adalah sebab yang mewajibkan shalat dan merupakan salah satu syarat shalat. Nabi ﷺ menjelaskan waktu-waktu lima shalat harian dalam lebih dari satu hadis. Ibnu Abbas (ra) meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "[Malaikat] Jibril mengimamiku dalam shalat di Baitullah pada dua [hari]." Kemudian beliau menyebutkan waktu-waktu shalat.

Waktu Shalat Dzuhur: Waktu shalat Dzuhur dimulai ketika matahari tergelincir dari meridiannya. Berakhirnya waktu Dzuhur adalah ketika bayangan suatu benda sama panjangnya dengan benda itu sendiri di sore hari.

Waktu Shalat Ashar: Awal waktu shalat Ashar adalah ketika bayangan suatu benda sama panjangnya dengan bendanya di sore hari. Berakhirnya waktu pilihan shalat Ashar adalah ketika bayangan suatu benda dua kali panjang bendanya. Namun jika diperlukan, shalat Ashar boleh dilakukan hingga [sebelum] matahari terbenam.

Waktu Shalat Maghrib: Awal waktu shalat Maghrib adalah ketika matahari terbenam. Waktu akhirnya adalah ketika bintang-bintang menjadi banyak dan berkelompok satu sama lain. Namun waktu akhir sesungguhnya, meskipun dimakruhkan menundanya hingga waktu ini, adalah ketika syafaq (cahaya merah di langit) telah hilang.

Waktu Shalat Isya: Awal waktunya adalah ketika syafaq telah menghilang. Waktu akhirnya adalah ketika setengah malam telah berlalu.

Waktu Shalat Subuh: Awal waktunya adalah pada fajar kedua [atau fajar yang sebenarnya] dan waktu akhirnya adalah saat matahari terbit.

Waktu Shalat di Negeri-Negeri yang Sangat Jauh di Utara atau Selatan: Negeri-negeri dengan lintang ekstrem dibagi menjadi tiga kategori: (1) Negeri-negeri yang berada antara 45° dan 48° lintang utara atau selatan dari ekuator; di negeri-negeri ini, tanda-tanda siang dan malam sangat jelas, baik sangat panjang maupun sangat pendek. (2) Negeri-negeri yang berada antara 48° dan 66° lintang utara atau selatan; di negeri-negeri ini, beberapa tanda siang dan malam tidak tampak selama beberapa bagian tahun. (3) Negeri-negeri dari lebih dari 66° lintang utara atau selatan hingga kutub-kutub; di negeri-negeri ini, tanda-tanda harian malam dan siang tidak terlihat selama periode waktu yang panjang dalam setahun.

Hukum-hukum bagi masing-masing negeri tersebut: Untuk negeri-negeri dalam kategori (1), wajib bagi penduduknya untuk melaksanakan shalat pada waktu-waktu yang telah ditentukan. Untuk negeri-negeri dalam kategori (3), tidak ada perbedaan pendapat bahwa penduduknya harus memperkirakan waktu-waktu shalat. Ini analogis dengan kasus memperkirakan waktu-waktu shalat di masa Dajjal. Dalam hadis, Nabi ﷺ ditanya berapa lama Dajjal akan berada di bumi. Dalam jawabannya, beliau berkata: "Sehari seperti setahun." Para sahabat bertanya: "Hari yang seperti setahun itu, apakah cukup bagi kami untuk shalat [dalam waktu] sehari semalam?" Beliau menjawab: "Tidak, kalian harus memperkirakan [waktu-waktu shalat]." (Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.) Untuk negeri-negeri dalam kategori kedua, waktu-waktu shalat selain Isya dan Subuh akan sama dengan kategori pertama. Sedangkan untuk shalat Isya dan Subuh, hukumnya sama dengan kategori ketiga.

Shalat berjamaah adalah salah satu perbuatan ketaatan yang paling penting kepada Allah dan salah satu ibadah yang paling agung. Shalat berjamaah menampilkan persatuan, kasih sayang, dan kesetaraan yang besar di antara kaum Muslim, saat mereka berkumpul bersama dalam pertemuan kecil lima kali sehari semalam, dengan cara yang mulia, di bawah kepemimpinan satu orang dan semua menghadap satu arah. Hati-hati bersatu dan tersucikan; kasih sayang dan hubungan diperkuat; dan perbedaan-perbedaan mengalir pergi.
content_ar:
من الأبواب الفقهية المهمة في عبادة الصلاة: أحكام الصلاة وشروطها وأركانها. وقد قال الله تعالى: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّلَاةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَابًا مَّوْقُوتًا﴾. فالصلاة فريضة مؤقتة لها أوقات محددة لا يصح أداؤها قبلها، وتُكره أو تحرم إقامتها في الأوقات المنهي عنها.

وأوقات الصلوات الخمس: الفجر من طلوع الفجر الصادق إلى طلوع الشمس، والظهر من زوال الشمس إلى أن يصير ظل كل شيء مثله، والعصر من انتهاء وقت الظهر إلى اصفرار الشمس، والمغرب من غروب الشمس إلى غروب الشفق الأحمر، والعشاء من انتهاء وقت المغرب إلى منتصف الليل في الأصح.

وأركان الصلاة التي لا تصح بدونها: القيام لمن يستطيع، وتكبيرة الإحرام، وقراءة الفاتحة، والركوع والاطمئنان فيه، والاعتدال عنه، والسجود والاطمئنان فيه، والجلوس بين السجدتين، والتشهد الأخير، والتسليم. فمن أخل بركن منها بطلت صلاته.
